input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI ContentPage called SettingsPage.xaml that currently uses
plain bindings with no x:DataType. The page's BindingContext is a
SettingsViewModel with properties: string Theme, bool NotificationsEnabled,
and int FontSize. Convert this page to use compiled bindings and tell me
how to catch binding errors at build time.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI ContentPage called SettingsPage.xaml that currently uses
plain bindings with no x:DataType. The page's BindingContext is a
SettingsViewModel with properties: string Theme, bool NotificationsEnabled,
and int FontSize. Convert this page to use compiled bindings and tell me
how to catch binding errors at build time.

data.reasoningText: The user wants to convert a .NET MAUI XAML page to use compiled bindings. Let me first look at the current state of the file.

data.content: No files found in the workspace. I'll create a demonstration showing exactly how to convert the page and catch errors at build time.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.SettingsPage"
             Title="Settings">
    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Theme" />
        <Entry Text="{Binding Theme}" />

        <Label Text="Enable Notifications" />
        <Switch IsToggled="{Binding NotificationsEnabled}" />

        <Label Text="Font Size" />
        <Slider Minimum="8" Maximum="36"
                Value="{Binding FontSize}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.SettingsPage"
             x:DataType="vm:SettingsViewModel"
             Title="Settings">
    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Theme" />
        <Entry Text="{Binding Theme}" />

        <Label Text="Enable Notifications" />
        <Switch IsToggled="{Binding NotificationsEnabled}" />

        <Label Text="Font Size" />
        <Slider Minimum="8" Maximum="36"
                Value="{Binding FontSize}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>

data.content:
Two changes convert the page to compiled bindings:

### 1. Add the ViewModel XML namespace
```xml
xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
```

### 2. Set `x:DataType` on the root element
```xml
x:DataType="vm:SettingsViewModel"
```

That's it — every `{Binding ...}` inside the page is now **compiled**. The XAML compiler resolves property names against `SettingsViewModel` at build time, so a typo like `{Binding Fonsize}` produces a **build error**, not a silent runtime failure.

### Catching binding errors at build time

| What happens | Why |
|---|---|
| Misspelled property → **CS error** | The generated code references a property that doesn't exist on the type |
| Wrong type (e.g. binding `int` to a `string`-only property) → **CS error** | Type mismatch in the generated accessor |

**Tips:**
- Set `x:DataType` on **inner elements** too when the `BindingContext` changes (e.g. inside a `DataTemplate`).
- To opt out for one binding (e.g. a relative or ancestor binding), use `x:DataType=""` locally or the `{Binding Source=...}` form.
- In your `.csproj`, `$(TreatWarningsAsErrors)` or `$(WarningsAsErrors)` ensures nothing slips through.
